meta.title: Sri Lanka District Specialties Dataset
meta.purpose: Location-triggered notification content and Culture-tab listing for the Tripsmart / Rawana Ceylon app. Categories: food, drink, dessert, craft, gem_mineral, agri_produce, textile. notification_text is ready-to-send push copy (<=140 chars).

items.name: Short eats (kade snacks)
items.description: Savory bites like pol roti, fish buns, and cutlets sold at street-corner 'kades' across the capital.
items.notification_text: You're in Colombo! Grab some short eats — the city's iconic street-corner snack culture.

items.name: Kottu roti
items.description: Chopped roti stir-fried with vegetables, egg, and meat on a hot griddle, a Colombo street-food icon.
items.notification_text: Craving something sizzling? Colombo is kottu roti's home turf — find a night-time kottu stall nearby.

items.name: Isso wade (prawn fritters)
items.description: Deep-fried lentil fritters topped with a whole prawn, classic evening snack at Galle Face Green.
items.notification_text: Evening walk on Galle Face Green? Don't miss isso wade — Colombo's beachside prawn fritters.

items.name: Lamprais
items.description: Dutch Burgher rice parcel with mixed meat curry, baked in a banana leaf; a Colombo Burgher-community classic.
items.notification_text: Try lamprais — a Dutch-Burgher rice specialty baked in banana leaf, rooted in old Colombo.

items.name: Colombo boutique batik
items.description: City boutiques and Barefoot-style stores sell hand-dyed batik textiles from island-wide artisans.
items.notification_text: Colombo's boutiques are a great stop for hand-dyed Sri Lankan batik textiles.

items.name: Colombo gem & jewellery district
items.description: Sea Street and the World Trade Center house Sri Lanka's biggest gem and jewellery trading hub.
items.notification_text: Near Sea Street? It's Colombo's historic gem and jewellery trading quarter.

items.name: Pettah market produce
items.description: Sri Lanka's largest open-air bazaar, famous for spices, dried goods, and fresh produce stalls.
items.notification_text: Pettah Market nearby — wander the spice and dried-goods stalls of Colombo's oldest bazaar.

items.name: Colombo-style milk toffee & wattalapam
items.description: Coconut-milk toffee and cardamom custard pudding widely sold in the capital's sweet shops.
items.notification_text: Pick up milk toffee or wattalapam from a Colombo sweet shop before you go.

items.name: Devon Avenue biryani
items.description: Colombo's biryani houses, many run by long-standing Muslim families, are famous for rich, spiced rice biryani.
items.notification_text: Colombo has some of the island's best biryani houses — worth a detour if you're nearby.

items.name: Elephant House ginger beer
items.description: A Colombo-founded soft drink brand whose ginger beer has been a Sri Lankan household staple since colonial times.
items.notification_text: Try a classic Elephant House ginger beer — a Colombo soft-drink institution since colonial times.

items.name: Wellawatte Tamil food street
items.description: Colombo's Wellawatte area is known for South Indian and Jaffna Tamil eateries, dosa, and filter coffee.
items.notification_text: Wellawatte is Colombo's go-to strip for South Indian dosa and filter coffee.

items.name: Indian sweet shops of Sea Street
items.description: Colombo's Sea Street and Bambalapitiya areas host long-running Indian sweet shops popular with locals.
items.notification_text: Colombo's older neighborhoods have long-running Indian sweet shops worth a stop.

items.name: Colombo antique & curio shops
items.description: The city's Galle Road antique shops trade in colonial-era furniture, brass, and curios sourced island-wide.
items.notification_text: Colombo's Galle Road antique shops are a good hunting ground for colonial-era curios.

items.name: Colombo hoppers & string hoppers
items.description: Local hopper shops across the city serve egg hoppers and string hoppers with a variety of curries.
items.notification_text: Colombo's hopper shops are a great evening stop for egg hoppers and string hoppers.

items.name: Negombo lagoon crab curry
items.description: Rich, spicy crab curry using mud crabs caught fresh from the Negombo lagoon.
items.notification_text: You're near Negombo Lagoon — try the crab curry made from crabs caught right here.

items.name: Negombo prawns
items.description: Jumbo lagoon and sea prawns, a signature catch of Negombo's fishing fleet.
items.notification_text: Negombo is prawn country — look for jumbo prawns fresh off the day's catch.

items.name: Dried fish (karawala)
items.description: Sun-dried fish prepared by Negombo's fishing communities, a staple flavor booster in local curries.
items.notification_text: Negombo's fish markets are known for sun-dried fish (karawala) — a local pantry staple.

items.name: Coconut toddy (ra)
items.description: Fermented sap tapped from coconut palms in the 'Coconut Triangle' spanning Gampaha.
items.notification_text: You're in the Coconut Triangle — ask about toddy (ra), the traditional palm-tapped drink.

items.name: Cane furniture (Weweldeniya)
items.description: The Weweldeniya area of Gampaha District is a traditional center for handwoven cane furniture.
items.notification_text: Weweldeniya nearby — a traditional hub for handwoven cane furniture.

items.name: Negombo Fish Market catch
items.description: One of Sri Lanka's largest fish markets, where the day's ocean catch is auctioned each morning.
items.notification_text: Negombo's fish market is buzzing at dawn — one of the island's biggest seafood auctions.

items.name: Ja-Ela / Katunayake seafood
items.description: Coastal towns near the airport serve fresh lagoon and sea catch to travelers passing through.
items.notification_text: Near the airport, Ja-Ela's coastal eateries serve fresh lagoon and sea catch.

items.name: Minuwangoda cinnamon
items.description: Parts of Gampaha District cultivate cinnamon as an extension of the island's coastal cinnamon belt.
items.notification_text: Gampaha's Minuwangoda area grows cinnamon as part of the island's coastal cinnamon belt.

items.name: Gampaha king coconut
items.description: The district's coconut-triangle plantations are a strong source of the sweet orange-shelled king coconut.
items.notification_text: Gampaha's plantations are a great place to grab a fresh king coconut.

items.name: Ja-Ela cashew snacks
items.description: Local shops around Ja-Ela sell roasted and spiced cashew nut snacks.
items.notification_text: Ja-Ela's local shops sell roasted, spiced cashew snacks worth trying.

items.name: Beruwala batik
items.description: Beruwala is one of Sri Lanka's oldest centers for hand-dyed batik cloth production.
items.notification_text: Beruwala nearby — one of the island's original batik-making towns.

items.name: Kalutara rambutan
items.description: The district is well known for its rambutan orchards, especially in season (June-August).
items.notification_text: In season, Kalutara's roadside stalls sell some of the island's best rambutan.

items.name: Kalutara mangosteen & durian
items.description: Home gardens around Kalutara commonly grow mangosteen and durian alongside rubber estates.
items.notification_text: Kalutara's gardens are known for mangosteen and durian — worth a roadside stop.

items.name: Rubber estates
items.description: Kalutara sits in Sri Lanka's rubber belt, with plantations visible along the Colombo-Galle route.
items.notification_text: You're passing through Sri Lanka's rubber belt — Kalutara's plantations line the coastal route.

items.name: Wadduwa & Beruwala seafood
items.description: Coastal towns in Kalutara District serve fresh reef and lagoon seafood at beachside eateries.
items.notification_text: Beachside in Wadduwa or Beruwala? Fresh seafood is the local specialty.

items.name: Kalutara Bodhiya sweetmeats
items.description: Vendors near the Kalutara Bodhiya stupa sell traditional Sinhala sweets to pilgrims.
items.notification_text: Visiting Kalutara Bodhiya? Try the traditional sweets sold by nearby vendors.

items.name: Panadura seafood curries
items.description: The coastal town of Panadura is known for its lagoon and sea fish curries.
items.notification_text: Panadura is known for its lagoon and sea fish curries — a coastal Kalutara specialty.

items.name: Kalutara cinnamon oil
items.description: Small distilleries in Kalutara process cinnamon leaf and bark into cinnamon oil.
items.notification_text: Kalutara has small distilleries producing cinnamon oil from local bark and leaf.

items.name: Beruwala fish market catch
items.description: Beruwala's fishing harbor is one of the busiest on the west coast, supplying fresh reef and deep-sea fish.
items.notification_text: Beruwala's harbor is one of the busiest fishing ports on the west coast.

items.name: Kandyan brass & silverware
items.description: Kandy is Sri Lanka's historic center for brass and silver metalwork — lamps, trays, and jewellery.
items.notification_text: You're in Kandy — the island's classic destination for handcrafted brass and silverware.

items.name: Dumbara mats (Henawala)
items.description: Woven wall-hanging mats from Henawala village in the Dumbara Valley, made by the Kinnara community from plant fiber.
items.notification_text: Near the Dumbara Valley — home of the Henawala weavers' famous Dumbara mats.

items.name: Kandyan lacquerware
items.description: Lathe-turned wooden items finished with colored lac, a craft historically centered on the Kandyan hills.
items.notification_text: Kandy's lacquerware — colorful lac-finished boxes and ornaments — makes a classic souvenir.

items.name: Kandyan wood carving
items.description: Intricately carved wooden furniture, masks, and figurines from Kandy-area workshops.
items.notification_text: Look out for Kandyan wood carving — an intricate craft tradition rooted in the old capital.

items.name: Kalu dodol
items.description: A dense, dark coconut-and-jaggery sweet, traditionally associated with Kandyan festive tables.
items.notification_text: Try kalu dodol — a rich coconut-jaggery sweet from the Kandyan tradition.

items.name: Betel leaf (bulath)
items.description: Kandy District farms produce betel leaf, traditionally offered as a mark of respect in Sinhalese culture.
items.notification_text: Betel leaf farming is part of Kandy's agricultural heritage — a symbol of respect in local custom.

items.name: High-grown Kandy tea
items.description: Kandy District mid-elevation estates produce a distinct 'mid-grown' Ceylon tea grade.
items.notification_text: You're in tea country — Kandy's mid-grown estates produce a distinct Ceylon tea style.

items.name: Pujapitiya brassware village
items.description: A village near Kandy specializing for generations in cast and hammered brass items.
items.notification_text: Pujapitiya nearby — generations of brass-casting craftsmanship.

items.name: Kandyan milk rice & sweetmeats
items.description: Traditional Kandyan New Year sweets — kevum, kokis, and mung kavum — are strongly associated with the region.
items.notification_text: Kandy is known for traditional New Year sweets like kevum and kokis — worth seeking out.

items.name: Kandy jaggery
items.description: Palm jaggery from the surrounding hill country is a common local sweetener sold in Kandy's markets.
items.notification_text: Kandy's markets sell hill-country palm jaggery — a natural local sweetener.

items.name: Kandy gem jewellery setting
items.description: Kandy's jewellers are known for setting Sri Lankan gemstones into traditional Kandyan-style jewellery.
items.notification_text: Kandy's jewellers specialize in setting local gems into traditional Kandyan-style jewellery.

items.name: Kandy area coffee & spices
items.description: Small hillside plots around Kandy grow coffee and spices alongside the region's tea.
items.notification_text: Hillsides around Kandy grow small-batch coffee and spices alongside its famous tea.

items.name: Kandy area passion fruit cordial
items.description: Small plantations in the Kandy hills also produce passion fruit for local cordial makers.
items.notification_text: Kandy's hills also grow passion fruit, used in local cordial.

items.name: Matale spice gardens
items.description: Cinnamon, pepper, cardamom, cloves, and nutmeg are grown and processed in Matale's famous spice gardens.
items.notification_text: You're in Sri Lanka's spice heartland — Matale's gardens grow cinnamon, pepper, cardamom, and more.

items.name: Pallehapuwita lacquer work
items.description: This Matale village is renowned island-wide for traditional lac-turned handicrafts.
items.notification_text: Pallehapuwita nearby — a village famous for traditional lacquerware craftsmanship.

items.name: Matale cocoa & spice arrack
items.description: Local cocoa cultivation and spice-infused arrack are specialties tied to the district's plantations.
items.notification_text: Matale grows cocoa and flavors arrack with local spices — worth seeking out.

items.name: Dambulla economic center produce
items.description: Dambulla hosts Sri Lanka's largest wholesale vegetable and fruit market, supplying the whole island.
items.notification_text: Dambulla's wholesale market is the island's biggest produce hub — a working slice of Sri Lankan agriculture.

items.name: Matale mangosteen & durian
items.description: The district's fertile hill country supports mangosteen and durian orchards similar to the wet zone south.
items.notification_text: Matale's orchards are known for mangosteen and durian in season.

items.name: Matale curry powder
items.description: The district's spice-processing tradition has made it a historic source of roasted Sri Lankan curry powder blends.
items.notification_text: Matale has long been a source of roasted Sri Lankan curry powder blends — a spice-trade legacy.

items.name: Matale cane basket weaving
items.description: Villages in Matale District also practice cane and reed basket weaving as a household craft.
items.notification_text: Matale's villages practice cane and reed basket weaving as a traditional household craft.

items.name: Sigiriya-area rice fields
items.description: The paddy fields surrounding Sigiriya, within Matale District, are part of the ancient dry-zone irrigation network.
items.notification_text: The paddy fields around Sigiriya are part of an ancient dry-zone irrigation network.

items.name: High-grown Ceylon tea
items.description: Nuwara Eliya's high-elevation estates produce Ceylon's most prized, delicately flavored tea.
items.notification_text: You're in Nuwara Eliya — home of Ceylon's finest high-grown tea. Visit an estate factory nearby.

items.name: Nuwara Eliya strawberries
items.description: The cool climate makes this district Sri Lanka's main strawberry-growing region.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya's cool climate grows the island's best strawberries — look for roadside farms.

items.name: Ambewela buffalo curd & dairy
items.description: The Ambewela farm area produces well-known buffalo curd and fresh dairy products.
items.notification_text: Near Ambewela — try the farm-fresh buffalo curd this area is known for.

items.name: Upcountry vegetables (leeks, carrots, cabbage)
items.description: Nuwara Eliya's temperate climate supports large-scale cultivation of temperate vegetables rare elsewhere on the island.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya grows Sri Lanka's leeks, carrots, and cabbage — a cool-climate farming hub.

items.name: Ambewela rainbow trout
items.description: Cold mountain streams around Ambewela support Sri Lanka's small rainbow trout farming industry.
items.notification_text: This is trout country — Ambewela's cold streams support Sri Lanka's rare trout farms.

items.name: Passion fruit & roses
items.description: Nuwara Eliya's gardens are known for passion fruit cordial and cut-flower rose cultivation.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya's gardens are famous for passion fruit and roses — a legacy of its colonial hill-station past.

items.name: Nuwara Eliya honey
items.description: Cool-climate apiaries around Nuwara Eliya produce a distinct highland honey.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya's cool-climate apiaries produce a distinct highland honey — worth tasting.

items.name: Nuwara Eliya blackberries & plums
items.description: Cool-climate orchards near Nuwara Eliya grow temperate fruit rare elsewhere in Sri Lanka.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya's cool orchards grow blackberries and plums — rare fruit for this tropical island.

items.name: Nuwara Eliya bottled spring water
items.description: The district's high-elevation springs are a source of several bottled mineral water brands.
items.notification_text: Nuwara Eliya's mountain springs supply several of Sri Lanka's bottled water brands.

items.name: Meetiyagoda moonstone mines
items.description: Meetiyagoda, near Ambalangoda, is Sri Lanka's principal source of blue moonstone.
items.notification_text: Meetiyagoda nearby — this is where Sri Lanka's famous blue moonstone is mined.

items.name: Ambalangoda traditional masks
items.description: Ambalangoda is the historic home of Sri Lankan devil-dance and folk theatre mask carving.
items.notification_text: You're in Ambalangoda — the birthplace of Sri Lanka's traditional devil-dance masks.

items.name: Beeralu lace
items.description: Bobbin lace-making (beeralu), introduced by the Portuguese, is a household craft along the Galle coast.
items.notification_text: Look for beeralu lace-makers along the Galle coast — a Portuguese-era craft still practiced by hand.

items.name: Galle ebony carvings
items.description: For centuries, Galle District artisans have carved ebony wood into elephants and figurines.
items.notification_text: Galle's craftsmen have carved ebony elephants and figurines for centuries — a classic souvenir here.

items.name: Koggala / Ahangama cinnamon peeling
items.description: This stretch of the Galle coast is part of Sri Lanka's historic 'Cinnamon Coast' cultivation belt.
items.notification_text: You're on the Cinnamon Coast — Koggala and Ahangama have peeled cinnamon for generations.

items.name: Koggala stilt-fishing catch
items.description: Iconic stilt fishermen near Koggala and Weligama catch small reef fish using a traditional technique.
items.notification_text: Watch for Galle's iconic stilt fishermen — a centuries-old fishing tradition unique to this coast.

items.name: Galle Fort short eats & bakeries
items.description: Dutch and colonial-era bakeries inside Galle Fort sell distinctive short eats and pastries.
items.notification_text: Inside Galle Fort? Its old Dutch-era bakeries serve some distinctive local short eats.

items.name: Galle Fort seafood platters
items.description: Restaurants inside the fortified old town specialize in grilled reef fish and prawn platters.
items.notification_text: Galle Fort's restaurants are known for grilled reef fish and prawn platters.

items.name: Hikkaduwa drum making
items.description: Hikkaduwa, on the Galle coast, is also a center for traditional drum crafting alongside Kandy.
items.notification_text: Hikkaduwa is also known for traditional drum-making, alongside its famous reef and surf.

items.name: Unawatuna seafood shacks
items.description: The beach town of Unawatuna, in Galle District, is known for casual beachfront seafood dining.
items.notification_text: Unawatuna's beach shacks are a great spot for casual fresh seafood.

items.name: Galle jewellery & gem cutting
items.description: Galle city has a long tradition of gem cutting and jewellery trade tied to its port history.
items.notification_text: Galle has a long gem-cutting and jewellery trade tradition tied to its old port history.

items.name: Galle spice-market blends
items.description: Shops near the Fort sell curated Sri Lankan spice blends aimed at visitors.
items.notification_text: Shops near Galle Fort sell curated Sri Lankan spice blends worth taking home.

items.name: Weligama beeralu lace
items.description: Weligama is one of the main household centers for handmade bobbin lace on the south coast.
items.notification_text: Weligama is a beeralu lace-making hub — watch artisans work by hand along the coast road.

items.name: Matara buffalo curd
items.description: Clay-pot buffalo curd is widely sold along Matara's coastal roads, a classic south-coast dessert.
items.notification_text: South-coast clay-pot buffalo curd is a Matara classic — pair it with kithul treacle.

items.name: Cinnamon (Matara belt)
items.description: Matara forms part of the southern cinnamon-growing belt that gave Ceylon cinnamon its global reputation.
items.notification_text: You're in Ceylon cinnamon country — Matara has cultivated it for centuries.

items.name: Coir & coconut-fiber products
items.description: Matara's coastal coconut groves support a coir rope and coconut-fiber craft industry.
items.notification_text: Matara's coconut groves feed a local coir craft industry — ropes, mats, and brushes.

items.name: Dondra-area seafood
items.description: Waters off Dondra, Sri Lanka's southernmost point, yield a strong daily catch of reef and deep-sea fish.
items.notification_text: Near Dondra Head, the island's southern tip — expect a strong daily seafood catch.

items.name: Matara curd & treacle sets
items.description: Roadside stalls along the Matara coastal highway sell clay-pot curd paired with kithul treacle.
items.notification_text: Matara's coastal highway stalls sell clay-pot curd with treacle — a classic road-trip stop.

items.name: Matara coconut treacle
items.description: Coconut flower sap treacle, distinct from kithul treacle, is produced in parts of Matara District.
items.notification_text: Matara produces coconut-flower treacle, a lesser-known cousin of kithul treacle.

items.name: Dikwella / Mirissa fresh tuna
items.description: Fishing harbors at Dikwella and Mirissa land large quantities of fresh tuna daily.
items.notification_text: Dikwella and Mirissa's harbors land fresh tuna daily — a south-coast fishing hub.

items.name: Buffalo curd (meekiri) & buffalo milk
items.description: Hambantota's dry-zone buffalo herds make it one of Sri Lanka's premier buffalo curd and milk sources.
items.notification_text: You've reached Hambantota — famed island-wide for buffalo milk and clay-pot curd. Worth the taste test!

items.name: Hambantota salt (Bundala salterns)
items.description: Coastal salt pans near Bundala have supplied Sri Lanka with sea salt for generations.
items.notification_text: Bundala's salt pans nearby — this stretch of coast has produced sea salt for generations.

items.name: Tissamaharama rice
items.description: The ancient Tissa tank irrigation system supports extensive paddy cultivation around Tissamaharama.
items.notification_text: Tissamaharama's ancient tanks still irrigate the rice paddies around you.

items.name: Yala-area wild honey
items.description: Communities bordering Yala National Park harvest wild forest honey from the surrounding scrubland.
items.notification_text: Near Yala? Local villages harvest wild forest honey from the surrounding scrub — a rare treat.

items.name: Dried fish (Hambantota coast)
items.description: The district's fishing villages produce sun-dried fish, a dry-zone staple ingredient.
items.notification_text: Hambantota's coast is known for sun-dried fish, a staple of dry-zone cooking.

items.name: Hambantota chena cultivation produce
items.description: Traditional slash-and-burn chena farming around Hambantota produces millet, maize, and pulses.
items.notification_text: Hambantota's chena farmers grow traditional millet, maize, and pulses using an age-old method.

items.name: Kirinda / Bundala dried seafood
items.description: Fishing hamlets near Kirinda and Bundala process and sell dried seafood along the coast road.
items.notification_text: Kirinda and Bundala's fishing hamlets sell dried seafood along the coast road.

items.name: Hambantota onion & chili farms
items.description: Dry-zone irrigation around Hambantota supports onion and chili cultivation alongside rice.
items.notification_text: Hambantota's dry-zone farms grow onion and chili alongside its famous rice paddies.

items.name: Tissamaharama toddy
items.description: Coconut and palmyrah toddy tapping is practiced in parts of the Tissamaharama area.
items.notification_text: Tissamaharama has its own toddy-tapping tradition using coconut and palmyrah palms.

items.name: Hambantota mat weaving
items.description: Coastal villages here practice traditional pandanus-leaf mat weaving.
items.notification_text: Hambantota's coastal villages practice traditional pandanus-leaf mat weaving.

items.name: Jaffna crab curry
items.description: A fiery, tamarind-and-chili-forward crab curry considered one of the peninsula's signature dishes.
items.notification_text: You've reached Jaffna — home of the peninsula's legendary fiery crab curry.

items.name: Odiyal kool
items.description: A thick seafood-and-palmyrah-root soup unique to Jaffna Tamil cuisine.
items.notification_text: Try odiyal kool — a rich seafood soup thickened with palmyrah root, unique to Jaffna.

items.name: Palmyrah toddy & hakuru jaggery
items.description: The palmyrah palm, the symbol of the north, yields toddy, jaggery (hakuru), and fruit pulp across Jaffna District.
items.notification_text: Jaffna is palmyrah country — try hakuru jaggery or fresh palmyrah fruit, the region's signature palm.

items.name: Karuthakolomban mango
items.description: A prized, intensely sweet mango variety native to the Jaffna Peninsula.
items.notification_text: In season, look for karuthakolomban — Jaffna's famously sweet native mango.

items.name: Jaffna red onions & chili
items.description: The dry sandy soil of the peninsula is ideal for red onion and chili cultivation, supplying much of the island.
items.notification_text: Jaffna's sandy soil grows much of Sri Lanka's red onions and chili — a farming backbone of the north.

items.name: Jaffna mutton curry
items.description: A rich, deeply spiced curry reflecting the strong South Indian Tamil influence on the region's cuisine.
items.notification_text: Jaffna's mutton curry carries strong South Indian Tamil spicing — a north-coast must-try.

items.name: Jaffna pomegranate & grapes
items.description: Home gardens in the peninsula are known for pomegranate and one of the island's few grape-growing traditions.
items.notification_text: Jaffna's home gardens are known for pomegranate and grapes — unusual for Sri Lanka's climate.

items.name: Vadai & Jaffna short eats
items.description: Lentil vadai and other Tamil-style short eats are a defining street-food category in Jaffna town.
items.notification_text: Grab a vadai in Jaffna town — the north's take on the island's short-eats tradition.

items.name: Jaffna palmyrah fruit pulp (panampazha)
items.description: Ripe palmyrah fruit pulp is turned into a sweet seasonal treat unique to the north.
items.notification_text: In season, try panampazha — sweet palmyrah fruit pulp unique to the Jaffna region.

items.name: Palmyrah root drink (odiyal)
items.description: A traditional Jaffna drink and flour made from the starchy root of the palmyrah palm.
items.notification_text: Odiyal, made from palmyrah root, is a traditional Jaffna specialty drink and flour.

items.name: Jaffna banana (Seeni Kesel)
items.description: A small, very sweet banana variety cultivated widely across the peninsula.
items.notification_text: Try seeni kesel — Jaffna's small, extra-sweet banana variety.

items.name: Jaffna curd
items.description: Jaffna's own version of buffalo curd, often paired with palmyrah jaggery instead of kithul treacle.
items.notification_text: Jaffna's curd is often paired with palmyrah jaggery instead of the south's kithul treacle.

items.name: Jaffna palmyrah ice cream / drinks
items.description: Modern vendors have adapted palmyrah fruit into ice cream and cold drinks popular with visitors.
items.notification_text: Try modern palmyrah-flavored ice cream or drinks — a fresh twist on a traditional Jaffna crop.

items.name: Kilinochchi paddy rice
items.description: Post-resettlement agriculture has made Kilinochchi a growing rice-producing district in the Vanni.
items.notification_text: Kilinochchi's paddy fields are part of the Vanni region's rice-growing revival.

items.name: Palmyrah products
items.description: Like the rest of the north, Kilinochchi's landscape is dotted with palmyrah palms used for jaggery and crafts.
items.notification_text: Palmyrah palms define this Vanni landscape — look for local jaggery and woven crafts.

items.name: Chili cultivation
items.description: Dry-zone farmland around Kilinochchi supports significant chili cultivation.
items.notification_text: Kilinochchi's dry-zone farms are a notable chili-growing area.

items.name: Vanni forest honey
items.description: The forested Vanni region around Kilinochchi is a source of wild forest honey.
items.notification_text: The Vanni forests around Kilinochchi are a source of wild honey.

items.name: Kilinochchi banana cultivation
items.description: Resettled farmland in Kilinochchi District has expanded banana cultivation in recent years.
items.notification_text: Kilinochchi's resettled farmland has expanded banana cultivation in recent years.

items.name: Kilinochchi dry chili
items.description: Sun-dried chili processing is a common small-scale trade in the district's farming villages.
items.notification_text: Kilinochchi's farming villages commonly process sun-dried chili.

items.name: Mannar palmyrah & grapes
items.description: Mannar Island shares the north's palmyrah tradition and also grows grapes in its arid soil.
items.notification_text: Mannar's arid soil supports both palmyrah palms and one of Sri Lanka's few grape-growing patches.

items.name: Mannar dried fish & mullet
items.description: The island's fishing communities are known for mullet fishing and sun-dried fish production.
items.notification_text: Mannar's fishing villages are known for mullet catches and sun-dried fish.

items.name: Mannar salt pans
items.description: Shallow lagoons around Mannar support traditional salt-pan harvesting.
items.notification_text: Mannar's shallow lagoons feed traditional salt-pan harvesting — worth a look near the coast.

items.name: Mannar baobab fruit
items.description: Mannar is home to Sri Lanka's rare baobab trees, whose tangy fruit pulp is a local curiosity.
items.notification_text: Mannar has Sri Lanka's rare baobab trees — their tangy fruit pulp is a local curiosity worth trying.

items.name: Mannar prawns
items.description: Mannar's lagoon and coastal waters are a source of wild-caught prawns.
items.notification_text: Mannar's lagoon waters are a source of wild-caught prawns.

items.name: Mannar cashew
items.description: Sandy scrubland on Mannar Island also supports small-scale cashew cultivation.
items.notification_text: Mannar's sandy scrubland also supports small-scale cashew cultivation.

items.name: Vavuniya cashew
items.description: The Vanni's dry-zone scrubland supports cashew cultivation around Vavuniya.
items.notification_text: Vavuniya's dry-zone farms are known for cashew cultivation.

items.name: Vavuniya mango & rice
items.description: The district produces mango and paddy rice as part of the wider Vanni agricultural belt.
items.notification_text: Vavuniya's farmland grows mango and rice — a key part of the Vanni's agriculture.

items.name: Vavuniya banana
items.description: Vanni farmland around Vavuniya includes notable banana cultivation.
items.notification_text: Vavuniya's Vanni farmland is also known for banana cultivation.

items.name: Vavuniya dairy
items.description: Cattle farming around Vavuniya supports a regional dairy and curd trade.
items.notification_text: Vavuniya's cattle farms supply a regional dairy and curd trade.

items.name: Vavuniya palmyrah handicrafts
items.description: As part of the Vanni, Vavuniya shares the north's palmyrah-leaf basket and mat weaving tradition.
items.notification_text: Vavuniya shares the Vanni's palmyrah-leaf basket and mat weaving tradition.

items.name: Mullaitivu lagoon fish & prawns
items.description: The district's undeveloped lagoons and coastline yield abundant fish and prawns.
items.notification_text: Mullaitivu's quiet lagoons are known for fresh fish and prawns.

items.name: Wild forest honey
items.description: Communities near Mullaitivu's forested interior harvest wild honey.
items.notification_text: Mullaitivu's forested interior is a source of wild forest honey.

items.name: Mullaitivu dried fish
items.description: Coastal villages process sun-dried fish from the district's rich lagoon and sea catch.
items.notification_text: Mullaitivu's coastal villages are known for sun-dried fish from their lagoon catch.

items.name: Mullaitivu paddy fields
items.description: Post-war agricultural recovery has expanded paddy cultivation in Mullaitivu District.
items.notification_text: Mullaitivu's paddy fields reflect the district's ongoing post-war agricultural recovery.

items.name: Mullaitivu cashew
items.description: Inland scrubland in Mullaitivu District supports small-scale cashew farming.
items.notification_text: Mullaitivu's inland scrubland supports small-scale cashew farming.

items.name: Trincomalee seer fish
items.description: The deep natural harbor of Trincomalee yields prized seer fish, popular grilled or curried.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's harbor is famous for seer fish — try it grilled or in a coastal curry.

items.name: Mud crab
items.description: Trincomalee's coastal waters and lagoons are a major source of large mud crabs.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's lagoons produce some of the island's biggest mud crabs.

items.name: Trincomalee cashew & mango
items.description: Dry-zone farmland inland from Trincomalee grows cashew and mango.
items.notification_text: Inland Trincomalee grows cashew and mango in its dry-zone farmland.

items.name: Nilaveli / Uppuveli seafood
items.description: The beach towns north of Trincomalee are known for fresh grilled seafood at beachside cafes.
items.notification_text: Nilaveli and Uppuveli are known for fresh grilled seafood right on the beach.

items.name: Trincomalee dried fish
items.description: The harbor town's fishing communities produce sun-dried fish as a local specialty.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's fishing communities are known for sun-dried fish.

items.name: Trincomalee sea salt
items.description: Coastal salterns near Trincomalee harvest natural sea salt.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's coast has traditional sea-salt harvesting operations.

items.name: Trincomalee cashew snacks
items.description: Local vendors process and sell roasted cashew from the district's inland plantations.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's inland plantations supply the roasted cashew sold by local vendors.

items.name: Trincomalee king coconut
items.description: Coastal groves around Trincomalee are a good source of fresh king coconut.
items.notification_text: Trincomalee's coastal groves are a great spot to grab a fresh king coconut.

items.name: Batticaloa lagoon prawns & mud crab
items.description: The large Batticaloa lagoon is a major source of prawns and mud crab for the region.
items.notification_text: Batticaloa's famous lagoon is a major source of prawns and mud crab.

items.name: Batticaloa cashew
items.description: The Eastern Province's dry-zone conditions make Batticaloa a notable cashew-growing district.
items.notification_text: Batticaloa's dry-zone farms are known for cashew cultivation.

items.name: Batticaloa rice & curd
items.description: Paddy cultivation and buffalo curd production are both significant in the district's agriculture.
items.notification_text: Batticaloa's countryside is known for both paddy rice and buffalo curd.

items.name: Batticaloa milk toffee
items.description: Local sweet shops in Batticaloa are known for their version of coconut milk toffee.
items.notification_text: Batticaloa's sweet shops have their own take on classic coconut milk toffee.

items.name: Batticaloa mat weaving
items.description: Coastal villages around the Batticaloa lagoon practice traditional reed and pandanus mat weaving.
items.notification_text: Villages around Batticaloa lagoon practice traditional reed and pandanus mat weaving.

items.name: Batticaloa king coconut
items.description: Lagoon-side groves around Batticaloa also grow king coconut for local sale.
items.notification_text: Batticaloa's lagoon-side groves grow king coconut for local sale.

items.name: Ampara paddy rice
items.description: Fed by the Gal Oya irrigation scheme, Ampara is one of Sri Lanka's largest rice-producing districts — the island's 'rice bowl'.
items.notification_text: You're in the 'rice bowl of Sri Lanka' — Ampara's Gal Oya-fed paddies supply rice island-wide.

items.name: Ampara cashew & mango
items.description: Alongside rice, the district's dry-zone farmland supports cashew and mango cultivation.
items.notification_text: Ampara's farms also grow cashew and mango alongside its famous rice paddies.

items.name: Arugam Bay seafood
items.description: The surf town of Arugam Bay serves fresh-caught reef fish and prawns at its beachfront eateries.
items.notification_text: Arugam Bay's beachfront cafes serve fresh reef fish — a surf-town seafood tradition.

items.name: Ampara coconut
items.description: Coastal parts of Ampara District contribute to Sri Lanka's coconut cultivation belt.
items.notification_text: Ampara's coastal belt also contributes to Sri Lanka's coconut cultivation.

items.name: Ampara honey
items.description: Forest fringes near Ampara's paddy lands support small-scale wild honey harvesting.
items.notification_text: Ampara's forest fringes support small-scale wild honey harvesting alongside its rice fields.

items.name: Ampara reed mat weaving
items.description: Villages near Ampara's wetlands practice traditional reed mat weaving.
items.notification_text: Villages near Ampara's wetlands practice traditional reed mat weaving.

items.name: Coconut Triangle coconut products
items.description: Kurunegala anchors Sri Lanka's 'Coconut Triangle', producing coconut oil, milk, and desiccated coconut.
items.notification_text: You're in the Coconut Triangle — Kurunegala is a heartland of Sri Lanka's coconut industry.

items.name: Traditional drum making
items.description: Communities around Kurunegala and Hodiyadeniya are known for handcrafting jack-wood traditional drums.
items.notification_text: This area is known for traditional drum-making — jack-wood drums crafted by hand.

items.name: Kurunegala cashew & king coconut
items.description: The district's plantations also produce cashew and the orange-shelled king coconut.
items.notification_text: Kurunegala's plantations grow cashew and the sweet, orange-shelled king coconut.

items.name: Ethagala-area betel & curd
items.description: Farms near the landmark Ethagala rock grow betel leaf and produce local curd.
items.notification_text: Near Ethagala rock, local farms grow betel leaf and produce fresh curd.

items.name: Kurunegala curd
items.description: Dairy farms around Kurunegala produce curd sold widely across the North Western Province.
items.notification_text: Kurunegala's dairy farms produce curd sold widely across the North Western Province.

items.name: Kurunegala mango
items.description: Orchards around Kurunegala grow several popular local mango varieties.
items.notification_text: Kurunegala's orchards grow several of the island's popular local mango varieties.

items.name: Kurunegala jackfruit dishes
items.description: Home kitchens in the district commonly prepare young and ripe jackfruit curries and desserts.
items.notification_text: Kurunegala's home kitchens are known for both savory and sweet jackfruit dishes.

items.name: Kalpitiya seafood & dried fish
items.description: Kalpitiya's fishing fleet is known for fresh catch and traditional dried fish preparation.
items.notification_text: Kalpitiya's fishing fleet is famous for fresh seafood and traditional sun-dried fish.

items.name: Puttalam salt
items.description: The Puttalam lagoon area hosts some of Sri Lanka's largest commercial salt-pan operations.
items.notification_text: Puttalam's lagoon salt pans are among the island's largest — a striking sight along the coast.

items.name: Puttalam cashew & coconut
items.description: The district's sandy soils support extensive cashew and coconut cultivation.
items.notification_text: Puttalam's sandy soils are well suited to cashew and coconut farming.

items.name: Puttalam prawns
items.description: Puttalam Lagoon is a significant source of farmed and wild-caught prawns.
items.notification_text: Puttalam Lagoon is a major source of prawns — both farmed and wild-caught.

items.name: Puttalam cashew snacks
items.description: Local processors roast and package cashew from the district's coastal plantations.
items.notification_text: Puttalam's coastal plantations supply locally roasted cashew snacks.

items.name: Puttalam mango
items.description: Orchards around Puttalam grow mango alongside the district's cashew and coconut.
items.notification_text: Puttalam's orchards also grow mango alongside its cashew and coconut.

items.name: Ancient heirloom red rice
items.description: Anuradhapura preserves traditional heirloom red rice varieties tied to the island's ancient irrigation civilization.
items.notification_text: Anuradhapura still grows heirloom red rice varieties tracing back to its ancient irrigation civilization.

items.name: Wood apple (beli)
items.description: The dry-zone district is known for wood apple, used in juices, jam, and traditional remedies.
items.notification_text: Look for wood apple (beli) drinks here — a dry-zone specialty used in juices and traditional remedies.

items.name: Traditional pottery
items.description: Anuradhapura District has a long-standing clay pottery tradition tied to the region's ancient heritage.
items.notification_text: This region has a long clay-pottery tradition dating back to its ancient civilization.

items.name: Tank-fed fish & curd
items.description: The district's many ancient irrigation tanks support freshwater fishing and dairy/curd production.
items.notification_text: Anuradhapura's ancient tanks support freshwater fish and dairy farming to this day.

items.name: Anuradhapura curd
items.description: Dry-zone dairy farms around the ancient city produce curd sold in the region's markets.
items.notification_text: Anuradhapura's dry-zone dairy farms produce curd sold in the region's local markets.

items.name: Anuradhapura palmyrah crafts
items.description: Palmyrah-leaf basketry and mat weaving are practiced in parts of the district.
items.notification_text: Parts of Anuradhapura District still practice palmyrah-leaf basketry and mat weaving.

items.name: Anuradhapura banana
items.description: Irrigated farmland around Anuradhapura supports banana cultivation alongside rice.
items.notification_text: Anuradhapura's irrigated farmland also supports banana cultivation alongside rice.

items.name: Polonnaruwa rice
items.description: Fed by the Parakrama Samudra reservoir, Polonnaruwa is one of Sri Lanka's principal rice-producing districts.
items.notification_text: Polonnaruwa's paddies, fed by the ancient Parakrama Samudra, make it a major rice-producing district.

items.name: Tank fishery catch
items.description: The district's many reservoirs support a significant freshwater tank-fishing industry.
items.notification_text: Polonnaruwa's reservoirs support a strong freshwater fishing tradition — look for tank-fresh fish.

items.name: Buffalo curd
items.description: Dairy buffalo herds around Polonnaruwa's tank country support local curd production.
items.notification_text: Polonnaruwa's tank country also produces its own buffalo curd.

items.name: Polonnaruwa stone-craft souvenirs
items.description: Local artisans near the ancient city sell stone-carved replicas inspired by Polonnaruwa's monuments.
items.notification_text: Local artisans near the ancient city sell stone-carved souvenirs inspired by Polonnaruwa's monuments.

items.name: Polonnaruwa cashew & mango
items.description: Dry-zone farmland around the ancient city also supports cashew and mango cultivation.
items.notification_text: Polonnaruwa's dry-zone farmland also grows cashew and mango alongside its famous rice.

items.name: Polonnaruwa reed & cane crafts
items.description: Villages near the tank country practice reed and cane weaving as a household craft.
items.notification_text: Villages in Polonnaruwa's tank country practice reed and cane weaving as a household craft.

items.name: Uva tea
items.description: Badulla anchors the Uva tea region, prized for a distinctive seasonal 'muscatel' character found nowhere else.
items.notification_text: You're in Uva tea country — Badulla's estates produce a rare seasonal 'muscatel' flavored tea.

items.name: Passion fruit
items.description: The Uva highlands around Badulla are a notable source of passion fruit and cordial.
items.notification_text: Badulla's highlands are known for passion fruit — look for fresh cordial along the road.

items.name: Bandarawela / Ella buffalo curd
items.description: The Bandarawela and Ella areas within Badulla District are noted for cool-climate buffalo curd.
items.notification_text: Near Ella or Bandarawela — try the cool-climate buffalo curd this area is known for.

items.name: Haputale coffee heritage
items.description: Before tea took over, Haputale in Badulla District was part of Ceylon's 19th-century coffee belt.
items.notification_text: Haputale was once part of Ceylon's original 19th-century coffee belt, before tea took over.

items.name: Welimada potatoes & vegetables
items.description: The Welimada area within Badulla District is a significant upcountry vegetable and potato growing zone.
items.notification_text: Welimada, in Badulla District, is a major upcountry potato and vegetable growing zone.

items.name: Badulla strawberries
items.description: Cooler pockets of Badulla District, near the Nuwara Eliya border, also grow strawberries.
items.notification_text: Cooler parts of Badulla, near the Nuwara Eliya border, also grow strawberries.

items.name: Badulla handloom weaving
items.description: Small handloom weaving cooperatives operate in parts of Badulla District.
items.notification_text: Small handloom weaving cooperatives operate in parts of Badulla District.

items.name: Wellawaya wild honey
items.description: Villages around Wellawaya in Monaragala District are known for harvesting wild bee honey.
items.notification_text: Wellawaya's villages are known for harvesting wild bee honey — a Monaragala specialty.

items.name: Monaragala cashew
items.description: The district's dry-zone chena farmland supports significant cashew cultivation.
items.notification_text: Monaragala's dry-zone farmland is a notable cashew-growing area.

items.name: Bibile cane crafts
items.description: Bibile, in Monaragala District, is a traditional center for handwoven cane products.
items.notification_text: Bibile nearby — a traditional center for handwoven cane crafts.

items.name: King coconut
items.description: Monaragala's plantations are a regional source of the orange-shelled king coconut.
items.notification_text: Monaragala grows plenty of king coconut — a refreshing local stop on a hot day.

items.name: Monaragala mee oil products
items.description: Traditional oil pressed from mee (Madhuca) tree seeds is still produced in parts of Monaragala District.
items.notification_text: Parts of Monaragala still press traditional mee-tree seed oil — an old rural craft.

items.name: Monaragala rice & millet
items.description: Traditional dry-zone chena farming in Monaragala grows rice, millet, and pulses.
items.notification_text: Monaragala's chena farms grow traditional dry-zone rice, millet, and pulses.

items.name: Monaragala mango orchards
items.description: Dry-zone orchards in Monaragala District also grow mango alongside cashew.
items.notification_text: Monaragala's dry-zone orchards also grow mango alongside its cashew.

items.name: Ratnapura gem mines
items.description: Known as the 'City of Gems', Ratnapura is Sri Lanka's historic center for sapphire, ruby, and cat's-eye mining.
items.notification_text: Welcome to the City of Gems — Ratnapura has mined sapphires, rubies, and cat's-eye for centuries.

items.name: Kithul treacle & jaggery
items.description: The wet-zone forests of Ratnapura are a traditional source of kithul palm treacle and jaggery.
items.notification_text: Ratnapura's forests are prime kithul country — try treacle or jaggery tapped straight from the palm.

items.name: Ratnapura gotu kola
items.description: Fresh gotu kola greens, foraged from home gardens, are a well-known local salad ingredient here.
items.notification_text: Ratnapura's home gardens are known for fresh-foraged gotu kola, a local salad staple.

items.name: Rubber & rice
items.description: Ratnapura District combines wet-zone rubber plantations with lowland rice cultivation.
items.notification_text: Ratnapura's landscape mixes rubber plantations with lowland rice paddies.

items.name: Elahera gem fields
items.description: Bordering areas of Ratnapura and Matale District form the Elahera gem-mining belt, known for corundum deposits.
items.notification_text: You're near the Elahera gem belt, known for rich corundum (sapphire/ruby) deposits.

items.name: Ratnapura durian & mangosteen
items.description: The wet-zone climate around Ratnapura supports durian and mangosteen orchards.
items.notification_text: Ratnapura's wet-zone climate is well suited to durian and mangosteen orchards.

items.name: Ratnapura gem-cutting workshops
items.description: Small lapidary workshops throughout the city cut and polish rough gemstones mined locally.
items.notification_text: Ratnapura's lapidary workshops cut and polish gems mined right here in the district.

items.name: Kithul jaggery & treacle ('Kithul country')
items.description: Kegalle is widely regarded as the heartland of Sri Lanka's kithul palm treacle and jaggery production.
items.notification_text: You're in Kithul Country — Kegalle is the heartland of Sri Lanka's kithul treacle and jaggery.

items.name: Molagoda pottery
items.description: The village of Molagoda along the Colombo-Kandy road is one of Sri Lanka's most famous pottery centers.
items.notification_text: Molagoda nearby — one of Sri Lanka's most famous traditional pottery villages.

items.name: Pinnawala-area produce
items.description: Farms around the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage grow jackfruit and other wet-zone crops feeding the elephants and locals alike.
items.notification_text: Near Pinnawala — local farms here grow jackfruit and other wet-zone crops.

items.name: Kegalle rubber & cocoa
items.description: The district's plantations produce both rubber and cocoa, part of Sri Lanka's historic export crops.
items.notification_text: Kegalle's plantations grow rubber and cocoa — long-standing export crops of the region.

items.name: Kegalle jackfruit chips
items.description: Home industries in Kegalle process jackfruit into chips and preserves.
items.notification_text: Kegalle's home industries are known for jackfruit chips and preserves.

items.name: Kegalle king coconut & curd
items.description: Plantations and dairy farms around Kegalle supply king coconut and fresh curd to the region.
items.notification_text: Kegalle's plantations and dairy farms supply king coconut and fresh curd to the region.

items.name: Kegalle cane furniture
items.description: Cane furniture weaving is practiced in villages around Kegalle, alongside its pottery tradition.
items.notification_text: Villages around Kegalle also weave cane furniture, alongside its pottery tradition.
